feed.title: Security Advisory for PHP packages hosted at packagist.org
feed.subtitle: Security Advisory for PHP packages hosted at packagist.org on GitHub

entry.title: [code16/sharp] code16 Sharp vulnerable to Cross Site Scripting (XSS)
entry.content:
code16 Sharp v9.6.6 is vulnerable to Cross Site Scripting (XSS) src/Form/Fields/SharpFormUploadField.php.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-61457
  • https://github.com/code16/sharp/issues/611
  • https://github.com/chimmeee/vulnerability-research/blob/main/CVE-2025-61457
  • https://github.com/code16/sharp/blob/6d106b05aa07c6b46f5de28f909b732e1bbcdc47/src/Form/Fields/SharpFormUploadField.php#L97
  • https://github.com/code16/sharp/releases/tag/v9.7.0
  • https://github.com/code16/sharp/commit/bf7fedf2086d86aac16194733a6385564e5cf124
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-9778-v769-qvjf

entry.title: [magento/community-edition] Magento vulnerable to path traversal
entry.content:
Magento versions 2.4.9-alpha1, 2.4.8-p1, 2.4.7-p6, 2.4.6-p11, 2.4.5-p13, 2.4.4-p14 and earlier are affected by an Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal') vulnerability that could result in a security feature bypass. An attacker could leverage this vulnerability to modify limited data. Exploitation of this issue does not require user interaction.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-49559
  • https://helpx.adobe.com/security/products/magento/apsb25-71.html
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-h4f4-gv6h-x824

entry.title: [magento/community-edition] Magento vulnerable to path traversal
entry.content:
Magento versions 2.4.9-alpha1, 2.4.8-p1, 2.4.7-p6, 2.4.6-p11, 2.4.5-p13, 2.4.4-p14 and earlier are affected by an Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal') vulnerability that could result in a security feature bypass. An attacker could leverage this vulnerability to modify limited data. Exploitation of this issue does not require user interaction.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-49559
  • https://helpx.adobe.com/security/products/magento/apsb25-71.html
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-h4f4-gv6h-x824

entry.content:
Magento versions 2.4.9-alpha1, 2.4.8-p1, 2.4.7-p6, 2.4.6-p11, 2.4.5-p13, 2.4.4-p14 and earlier are affected by an Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal') vulnerability that could result in a security feature bypass. entry.title: [magento/project-community-edition] Magento vulnerable to path traversal
entry.content:
Magento versions 2.4.9-alpha1, 2.4.8-p1, 2.4.7-p6, 2.4.6-p11, 2.4.5-p13, 2.4.4-p14 and earlier are affected by an Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal') vulnerability that could result in a security feature bypass. An attacker could leverage this vulnerability to modify limited data. Exploitation of this issue does not require user interaction.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-49559
  • https://helpx.adobe.com/security/products/magento/apsb25-71.html
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-h4f4-gv6h-x824

entry.title: [magento/community-edition] Magento has incorrect authorization issue that leads to arbitrary file system read
entry.content:
Magento versions 2.4.9-alpha1, 2.4.8-p1, 2.4.7-p6, 2.4.6-p11, 2.4.5-p13, 2.4.4-p14 and earlier are affected by an Incorrect Authorization vulnerability that could result in a security feature bypass. An attacker could leverage this vulnerability to bypass security measures and gain unauthorized read access. Exploitation of this issue does not require user interaction, and scope is unchanged.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-49556
  • https://helpx.adobe.com/security/products/magento/apsb25-71.html
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-7hrj-3c9x-xv5h

entry.title: [magento/community-edition] Magento has incorrect authorization issue that leads to arbitrary file system read
entry.content:
Magento versions 2.4.9-alpha1, 2.4.8-p1, 2.4.7-p6, 2.4.6-p11, 2.4.5-p13, 2.4.4-p14 and earlier are affected by an Incorrect Authorization vulnerability that could result in a security feature bypass. An attacker could leverage this vulnerability to bypass security measures and gain unauthorized read access. Exploitation of this issue does not require user interaction, and scope is unchanged.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-49556
  • https://helpx.adobe.com/security/products/magento/apsb25-71.html
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-7hrj-3c9x-xv5h
